# ontology-extractor

Provider-agnostic TypeScript library for extracting a draft TBox from document corpora.

## Core import

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```typescript
import { OntologyExtractor } from "ontology-extractor";
```

The core entrypoint does not import a provider SDK. Implement `LlmAdapter` and `EmbeddingAdapter`, or use the optional LangChain bridge.

## Extraction accuracy

Use `extraction.accuracy` to control how selective the LLM should be while it extracts candidates from each chunk.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```typescript
const extractor = new OntologyExtractor({
  llm,
  embeddings,
  extraction: { accuracy: "high" },
});
```

The presets are `low`, `medium`, and `high`; the default is `medium`. `high` asks for only the most important concepts and relations and caps each chunk to fewer candidates. This happens before deduplication and canonicalization, while `output.supportCountThreshold` only filters the final emitted ontology.

## Prompt overrides

Override the prompts used by extraction and ambiguous canonicalization directly when creating the extractor. `systemPrompt` replaces the system instruction; `prompt` can be a static string or a function that receives the typed runtime input for each LLM call.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```typescript
const extractor = new OntologyExtractor({
  llm,
  embeddings,
  extraction: {
    systemPrompt: "Extract only GMP process ontology candidates.",
    prompt: (chunk) => JSON.stringify({
      documentId: chunk.documentId,
      chunkIndex: chunk.chunkIndex,
      text: chunk.text,
    }),
  },
  canonicalization: {
    systemPrompt: "Decide whether the candidate is a synonym, subtype, or new GMP type.",
    prompt: ({ candidate, neighbors }) => JSON.stringify({ candidate, neighbors }),
  },
});
```

The structured-output schemas and validators still apply, so custom prompts must ask the model to return the same candidate or verdict shape.

## LangChain bridge

Install a compatible `@langchain/core` package and import the separate subpath.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```typescript
import {
  LangChainEmbeddingAdapter,
  LangChainLlmAdapter,
} from "ontology-extractor/langchain";
```

`LangChainLlmAdapter` forwards JSON Schema to `withStructuredOutput`. `LangChainEmbeddingAdapter` requires the configured embedding dimension because LangChain's generic embedding interface does not expose it reliably. Cancellation rejects the adapter call promptly; some provider embedding clients may continue their underlying HTTP request because the LangChain embedding interface does not accept an `AbortSignal`.

## Interoperable import export

Convert the internal draft ontology into the import model used by compatible ontology systems.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```typescript
import { toOntologyImportModel } from "ontology-extractor";

const importModel = toOntologyImportModel(ontology, {
  name: "customer-support",
  namespace: "customer-support",
  description: "Ontology extracted from customer support procedures.",
});
```

The extractor requests typed attributes, primary and label attributes, categories, relationship cardinalities, and optional visual metadata when those values are supported by the source text. Existing adapters that return only `attributes: string[]` remain supported. During export, legacy attributes use `STRING` and conservative `false` flags. Every entity receives a valid primary key: the serializer prefers the extracted PK, then an existing `...Id` attribute, then the first attribute; if none exists, it creates a required and indexed `<entityName>Id` string attribute. Unsupported cardinalities, categories, and visual values are omitted rather than invented.

The export includes ontology metadata, categories, variables, entities, and relationships. Access-control sections such as `userGroups`, `securityProfiles`, and `policies` are intentionally omitted because they are not part of the extracted TBox and cannot be inferred safely from general ontology documents.
